disease_burden_data.name: CDC Tick Bite Data Tracker – ER Visits (2026 Spring Surge)
disease_burden_data.description: National syndromic surveillance of ED visits for tick bites. South Central region (TX, OK, AR, LA) did NOT see the same extreme spike as Midwest/Northeast, but still elevated overall.
disease_burden_data.notes: April 2026: 71 per 100,000 ER visits nationally (highest since 2017). Midwest (incl. MO) hit 137 per 100,000 — almost double 2025.

disease_burden_data.name: Missouri ER Visits for Tick Bites (2026)
disease_burden_data.description: Midwest region (incl. Missouri) saw sharp rise: 39 per 100,000 in March → 137 per 100,000 in April 2026.
disease_burden_data.notes: Directly tied to Lone Star tick activity and AGS warnings.

disease_burden_data.name: Missouri Alpha-gal Syndrome Burden
disease_burden_data.description: Official DHSS estimate: 7,300–34,400 Missourians affected since 2021. New 2026 bill makes AGS reportable.
disease_burden_data.notes: University Health (KC) opened dedicated AGS hotline due to rising cases.

disease_burden_data.name: Oklahoma Alpha-gal Syndrome (New Mandatory Reporting)
disease_burden_data.description: SB 1644 signed May 2026 — AGS becomes reportable Nov 1, 2026. Seroprevalence ~35% (military recruits); potential 70,000–140,000 full AGS cases.
disease_burden_data.notes: Oklahoma is one of the top 3 most impacted states nationally. Multiple personal reports of repeated ER visits for anaphylaxis.

disease_burden_data.name: Texas Tickborne Disease Cases (DSHS 2020–2026)
disease_burden_data.description: Low Lyme (21 cases in 2024, 19 in 2025 YTD); Ehrlichiosis steady (13–14 cases/year). Lone Star tick widespread.
disease_burden_data.notes: No statewide AGS count yet, but Texas is in the high Lone Star tick range → expected high AGS burden.

disease_burden_data.name: CDC MMWR Alpha-gal Geographic Distribution (2017–2022)
disease_burden_data.description: Suspected AGS cases heavily clustered in MO, OK, AR, TX-adjacent counties (high per-capita rates).
disease_burden_data.notes: South/Midwest hotspot confirmed — fills the 'empty South' gap on traditional Lyme maps.

disease_burden_data.name: Military Recruit Seroprevalence Study (Alpha-gal Antibody)
disease_burden_data.description: OK ~35%, MO ~29%, AR high. Tracks Lone Star tick range closely.
disease_burden_data.notes: Shows massive undercounted exposure in TX/OK/MO region.

generated_for: thisticksmeoff — Texas / Oklahoma / Missouri infection & ER burden data
notes_for_pipeline: Overlay CDC ER rates (Midwest spike includes MO) + state AGS estimates. Texas has hard case counts from DSHS. South is AGS/Ehrlichia-heavy, not Lyme-heavy — this data makes that visible.
